name: Nominated contacts
pageTitle: Nominated Contacts - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Contacts
heading: Nominated contacts (optional)
bodyCopy: Nominate up to 5 contacts. They will be notified if your consignment is chosen for inspection.

structure.govukComponent: Caption (govuk-caption-xl, id=page-secondary-title, inside the fieldset legend)
Fieldset legend as page heading (govuk-fieldset__heading + govuk-heading-xl, id=page-primary-title)

structure.text: Nominate up to 5 contacts. They will be notified if your consignment is chosen for inspection.
structure.govukComponent: Body text (govuk-body); wrapped in div#nominated-contact-page-hint and referenced by the fieldset's aria-describedby

structure.text: Column headers: Name | Email address | Mobile number | (visually hidden) Remove contact details
structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table govuk-!-margin-bottom-2, id=nominated-contacts-table), inside a div.govuk-!-margin-bottom-7.table-responsive wrapper

structure.text: One read-only row per entry in the view model's nominatedContacts array: name, email, telephone as plain text plus a Remove button (name=remove-nominated-contact, value=<index>). NOTE: the view model DEFAULTS this array to [{}], so exactly one BLANK row renders even when the draft holds no contacts at all — see nonStandardPatterns.
One editable entry row: Name / Email address / Mobile number text inputs. Gated on showNominatedContactInput (true while fewer than 5 contacts are saved).
structure.govukComponent: Text input (govuk-input) x3, each wrapped in govuk-form-group govuk-!-margin-bottom-0
Non-standard link-button (submit button styled as a link); sits INSIDE the fieldset, within the table-responsive wrapper, after the table. Also gated on showNominatedContactInput.

govukComponents.usage: Wraps the contacts group (legend + hint + table + Add another person; the save buttons sit OUTSIDE it). Legend contains the caption + H1 (standard GOV.UK legend-as-page-heading pattern). aria-describedby="nominated-contact-page-hint". The fieldset element itself carries only class=govuk-fieldset and aria-describedby — no other attributes.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 (input-phase snapshot; eval over document.querySelectorAll('[aria-describedby]') returned FIELDSET->nominated-contact-page-hint). Re-verified independently this pass.

govukComponents.usage: Section caption "Contacts" above the H1, id=page-secondary-title
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50; re-confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 58 (eval on .govuk-caption-xl returned exactly "Contacts")

govukComponents.usage: H1 "Nominated contacts (optional)", id=page-primary-title, also carries govuk-fieldset__heading
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 (main.innerText begins "Contacts\nNominated contacts (optional)"); H1 independently re-confirmed in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 58 (document.querySelector('h1').textContent === 'Nominated contacts (optional)'). Both pointers re-verified this pass — right page, no doubt.

govukComponents.usage: Lists saved contacts and hosts the editable entry row. id=nominated-contacts-table. Column headers (th id=name-header|email-header|phone-header, scope=col) double as the accessible labels for the inputs via aria-labelledby. The fourth th (the actions column) has NO id and carries only the visually hidden text. The table ALSO carries its own aria-describedby="nominated-contact-page-hint" — the same hint the fieldset already references (see nonStandardPatterns).
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — eval returned table class="govuk-table govuk-!-margin-bottom-2" id="nominated-contacts-table" aria-describedby="nominated-contact-page-hint"; th enumeration returned [name-header|col|Name, email-header|col|Email address, phone-header|col|Mobile number, (no id)|col|Remove contact details]

govukComponents.usage: Name (width-20), Email address (no width modifier), Mobile number (width-20). Each wrapped in a govuk-form-group with govuk-!-margin-bottom-0. None has a label, a hint, a maxlength or a required attribute.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — full attribute enumeration re-run independently this pass over document.querySelectorAll('main input[type=text],main input[type=email],main input[type=tel]'); exact attribute lists reproduced in the fields[] entries below.

govukComponents.usage: "Save and return to hub" (id=save-and-return-button, name=save-and-return-button, value="Save and return to hub") and "Save and continue" (id=button-save-and-continue, name=button-save-and-continue, value="Save and continue"). Both type=submit with data-module="govuk-button" and data-prevent-double-click="true". "Save and continue" is the ONLY control on this page any automated test ever drives — it is located by role+name, corroborating the accessible name.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — the action log's locator resolution returned verbatim: <button type="submit" value="Save and continue" data-module="govuk-button" id="button-save-and-continue" name="button-save-and-continue" data-prevent-double-click="true" class="govuk-button govuk-!-margin-right-1 govuk-!-margin-bottom-4">Save and continue</button>. Corroborated /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/NominatedContactsPage.ts:6-8 — page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Save and continue' }) (re-read in full this pass; the file is exactly 9 lines).

govukComponents.usage: "Back" link, id=back-link, href="#" — JS-driven history back. Sits in IPAFFS chrome, not inside main.
govukComponents.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 58 — eval returned exactly: Back. Re-verified character-for-character this pass.

govukComponents.usage: "Cancel and return to hub" -> /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/:draftId/overview (observed with draftId DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990), inside a
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — eval returned exactly: Cancel and return to hub

govukComponents.usage: Screen-reader-only text "Remove contact details" inside the fourth (actions) column header.
govukComponents.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 (th enumeration + main.innerText, which renders the string on its own line); template /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/nominatedContact.html:60-62

govukComponents.usage: Not rendered in either observed snapshot (no error state exercised). No trace and no test has ever put this page into an error state, so the error-summary rendering on this page is entirely unverified.
govukComponents.evidence: Absence re-confirmed independently this pass by eval (document.querySelectorAll('.govuk-error-summary').length === 0) in BOTH trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 and trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 58; no error-state coverage exists in the QA repo (see openQuestions — zero tests fill any field on this page).

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A real <button type="submit"> styled to look like a text link. Used for the per-row "Remove" action and the "Add another person" action. Both post the form back to the server; add/remove are server round-trips, not client-side DOM manipulation.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a Design System class. A submit button painted as a link is the exact affordance mismatch the DS warns about — it reads as navigation but mutates state. The rebuild should not carry this class over.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button--secondary for "Add another person"; for per-row "Remove" either govuk-button--secondary or, better, restructure the whole page as a Summary list with Remove/Change actions, or the DS "Add another" pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 (attribute enumeration). Never exercised by any test — no page object locator exists for either button.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The "Add another person" button carries a bare `govuk` class that is not a real govuk-frontend class and has no styling contract.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Looks like a truncated/typo class (e.g. an unfinished `govuk-button`). Dead or accidental markup — evidence the page drifted from the DS rather than being built to it. Do not replicate.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — attribute enumeration returned exactly: class="link-button govuk" id="add-nominated-contact" type="submit" name="add-nominated-contact". Re-verified this pass.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: #remove-nominated-contact0 has class="link-button" (no govuk-button class) yet still carries data-module="govuk-button" and has been initialised by the govuk-frontend JS (data-govuk-button-init="" present in the rendered DOM). #add-nominated-contact, the other link-button, does NOT carry data-module.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The govuk-frontend Button JS is being attached to an element that is not a DS Button, and inconsistently so across the two link-buttons on the same page. It gives the Remove button DS behaviours (e.g. double-click prevention semantics, space-key handling) that the visually identical Add button lacks. Incoherent — a symptom of the page being assembled from half-applied DS pieces.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use a real govuk-button (+ its data-module) or a real link — not a link-styled button with Button JS bolted on. Whatever the rebuild picks, apply it consistently to both actions.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — Remove: class="link-button" id="remove-nominated-contact0" name="remove-nominated-contact" type="submit" value="0" data-module="govuk-button" data-govuk-button-init=""; Add: class="link-button govuk" id="add-nominated-contact" type="submit" name="add-nominated-contact" (no data-module, no init). Both attribute lists re-enumerated this pass. Source: nominatedContact.html:79-84 hardcodes data-module="govuk-button" on the Remove button.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: aria-describedby="nominated-contact-page-hint" duplicated on BOTH the fieldset and the table
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The hint paragraph is referenced twice — once by <fieldset class="govuk-fieldset"> and again by <table id="nominated-contacts-table">. Only two elements on the page carry aria-describedby, and both point at the same hint.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A screen reader entering the fieldset and then the table can announce the same hint text twice. The DS pattern references a hint once, from the fieldset. Redundant and mildly hostile to assistive tech; invisible to axe (a valid idref is a valid idref).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Reference the hint once from the fieldset only. If the table needs its own description, give it a distinct caption/summary.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — eval over document.querySelectorAll('[aria-describedby]') returned exactly 2 elements: ["FIELDSET->nominated-contact-page-hint", "TABLE#nominated-contacts-table->nominated-contact-page-hint"]. Re-verified this pass.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific wrapper around the table, presumably providing horizontal scroll / responsive behaviour on narrow viewports.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom CSS outside the DS toolbox, needed only because a data-entry form was laid out as a wide table. If the rebuild drops the table layout, this wrapper becomes unnecessary.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None needed — a Summary list plus a one-field-per-line entry page is responsive without a custom wrapper.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — eval returned className "govuk-!-margin-bottom-7 table-responsive"

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Form inputs labelled by aria-labelledby="name-header|email-header|phone-header" — no <label> elements anywhere on the page
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The three text inputs have no <label>. Their accessible names come from the table's <th> column headers via aria-labelledby.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Significant deviation. Every DS text input requires its own govuk-label. Borrowing the column header means no per-field hint text is possible, error messages have no label to sit under, and the association breaks entirely if the table layout is ever reflowed on mobile. This is the strongest signal that the table-as-form layout is the root problem. NOTE: this pattern passes an axe WCAG 2.1 A/AA scan at a zero-violation threshold — axe accepts aria-labelledby as a valid accessible name. Automated a11y green does NOT mean DS-compliant here.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A real govuk-label per input (visually hidden if the visual design demands it), or drop the table and use standard stacked Text inputs with labels and hints.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — document.querySelectorAll('main label').length === 0 (re-verified this pass; also 0 in trace e10b7cd5... action 58), and each input's re-enumerated attribute list contains aria-labelledby but no id-matched label. Axe-clean claim: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/accessibility/ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:123 scans this page via checkPageAccessibility, and /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/utils/accessibility-utils.ts:21-22 sets maxViolations: 0, failOnViolations: true — a passing corpus therefore implies zero WCAG A/AA violations on this page.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: govuk-table used as a form layout container (inputs inside <td>)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A DS component used in a non-standard way — the table mixes read-only saved rows with a live data-entry row containing three inputs and reuses <th> as labels.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The DS Table is for tabular data, not form layout. Mixing saved records and an entry row in one table conflates two different jobs and forces the aria-labelledby hack above.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list (saved contacts, each with a Remove action) + a separate "Add a contact" page/section of stacked Text inputs — the DS "Add another" pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 (inputs are nested in td.govuk-table__cell); template nominatedContact.html:66-95 — {{#each nominatedContacts}} rows then {{#if showNominatedContactInput}} entry row, all inside one <tbody>

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: PHANTOM CONTACT ROW — the view model defaults nominatedContacts to [{}], so a blank removable row renders when the draft has NO contacts (CORRECTED THIS PASS)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Both observed traces render a contact row whose Name/Email/Mobile cells are entirely empty (whitespace only) yet which still offers a Remove button for index 0. THE CAUSE IS A VIEW-LAYER DEFAULT, NOT PERSISTED DATA. /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/transformers/notification_to_view/importer/nominated_contact.js:4 reads `nominatedContacts: _.get(notification, 'partOne.nominatedContacts', [{}])` — the default is an array containing one EMPTY OBJECT. The template's {{#each nominatedContacts}} (nominatedContact.html:67) therefore emits one all-blank row with Remove value="0" whenever the draft holds no contacts. Line 5 of the same transformer computes `showNominatedContactInput` from the SAME path but with a DIFFERENT default (`[]`) — the two defaults disagree, which is precisely why you see 1 phantom row + 1 entry row = 2 tbody rows on an empty draft.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Two bugs in two adjacent lines. (a) The mismatched defaults ([{}] vs []) mean the row count and the gating logic disagree about what an empty draft contains. (b) The phantom row offers a working Remove button for a contact that does not exist — pressing it posts remove-nominated-contact=0 against an absent array. It also presents an uncontactable ghost contact to the user for no reason. Note this row is NOT evidence of a data-quality problem in the draft store, and NOT evidence that the validator can be bypassed — the earlier reading of this spec drew both of those conclusions and both were wrong.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: n/a — a view-model defaulting bug. In the rebuild, default an empty contact list to [] everywhere and render zero rows (or an explicit empty state), and derive row-rendering and input-gating from ONE source of truth.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: RENDER (confirmed): trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — the first tbody row's three td.govuk-table__cell contain only whitespace, the fourth contains "Remove"; button#remove-nominated-contact0 value="0" present; tbody row count === 2. Reproduced in trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 58 (tbody row count === 2) on a different draft (DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990 vs DRAFT.GB.2026.1526011) — consistent with a code-path default rather than draft data. CAUSE (inferred, read first-hand this pass): transformers/notification_to_view/importer/nominated_contact.js:4-5; views/importer/nominatedContact.html:67 ({{#each nominatedContacts}}) and :90 ({{#if showNominatedContactInput}}).

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Disables native browser validation so server-side validation owns all error messaging. Single form; wraps the whole page including both save buttons.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This is actually the DS-recommended approach — noted for completeness, not as a defect.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — document.querySelectorAll('main form').length === 1 and form.hasAttribute('novalidate') === true (re-verified this pass)

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.conditionalOn: Rendered while showNominatedContactInput is true — i.e. fewer than 5 contacts saved (transformers/notification_to_view/importer/nominated_contact.js:5; gated in nominatedContact.html:90). The at-capacity (5-contact) render has NEVER been observed — no trace ever saved a contact — so the disappearance is `inferred` from source, not `confirmed`.
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — attribute enumeration returned exactly: class="govuk-input govuk-input--width-20 " id="name" name="name" type="text" value="" aria-labelledby="name-header" autocomplete="name". No required attribute, no maxlength, no label. Re-enumerated independently this pass. NOT-required corroborated by test behaviour: the CHED-PP workflows click Save and continue with this field blank and the journey proceeds to /documents/page-1 (/Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:448 and :871; the action-50 log records navigation to .../DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990/documents/page-1). No page-object locator exists for this input — no test has ever typed into it. MANDATORINESS (legacy): required=false is correct at the model layer for CHED-PP — the schema places NO CHED-PP @NotNull on the list (PartOne.java:641-644 has only @NotEmpty for the transporter/EU-CVEDA group) and NominatedContact.java:15-21 gives name/telephone/email no annotations at all. Name becomes required ONLY as a frontend conditional-add rule when a contact is being added (nominated_contact.js:45-58: `.when(...) then Joi.object({ name: Joi.required() })`) — a policy the rebuild must re-implement deliberately, not a hard model constraint.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.conditionalOn: Same as name — gated on showNominatedContactInput (< 5 contacts saved); at-capacity render never observed.
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — attribute enumeration returned exactly: class="govuk-input  " id="email" name="email" type="email" value="" aria-labelledby="email-header" autocomplete="email" spellcheck="false". No width modifier, no required, no maxlength, no label. NOT-required corroborated by ched-pp-workflows.ts:448. No page-object locator exists; ChedPPNotificationConfig has no nominated-contact email field (contrast billingEmail at ched-pp-workflows.ts:127).

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Mobile number
fields.conditionalOn: Same as name — gated on showNominatedContactInput (< 5 contacts saved); at-capacity render never observed.
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — attribute enumeration returned exactly: class="govuk-input govuk-input--width-20 " id="telephone" name="telephone" type="tel" value="" aria-labelledby="phone-header" autocomplete="tel". No required, no maxlength, no pattern, no label. NOT-required corroborated by ched-pp-workflows.ts:448. No page-object locator exists; ChedPPNotificationConfig has no nominated-contact telephone field (contrast billingTelephone at ched-pp-workflows.ts:128).

fields.name: remove-nominated-contact
fields.label: Remove
fields.conditionalOn: One per entry in the view model's nominatedContacts array (nominatedContact.html:67 {{#each}}). Because that array defaults to [{}], exactly one Remove button renders even on a draft with no contacts — which is the only case ever observed. Server-side the posted index is bounded 0..4 (nominated_contact.js:41).
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — attribute enumeration returned exactly: class="link-button" id="remove-nominated-contact0" name="remove-nominated-contact" type="submit" value="0" data-module="govuk-button" data-govuk-button-init="". Never clicked by any test — no page-object locator exists, so the remove behaviour is unverified beyond its markup.

fields.name: add-nominated-contact
fields.label: Add another person
fields.conditionalOn: Gated on showNominatedContactInput — same flag as the entry row (nominatedContact.html:131; transformer :5). So it silently disappears at 5 saved contacts. `inferred` from source; never observed at capacity.
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — attribute enumeration returned exactly: class="link-button govuk" id="add-nominated-contact" type="submit" name="add-nominated-contact". No value attribute, no data-module. Never clicked by any test — no page-object locator exists, so the add/round-trip behaviour is unverified beyond its markup. Server-side the payload key is validated as Joi.string().valid('') (nominated_contact.js:42), consistent with a valueless submit button.

fields.name: crumb
fields.label: (hidden) CSRF token
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — input[name=crumb][type=hidden], first child of the form, no id, no class. CSRF protection; the rendered value is a live token and is REDACTED here. (A second crumb input exists in the page footer's separate cookies form — not part of this page's data model.)

fields.name: etag
fields.label: (hidden) optimistic-concurrency ETag
fields.evidence: trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50 — input[name=etag][type=hidden], value="0000000000BD29E1" INCLUDING the literal surrounding double quotes (the eval returned the value as \"0000000000BD29E1\"). Carries the draft's version for optimistic concurrency on save; schema at nominated_contact.js:44 (ETAG_SCHEMA).

validationMessages.trigger: A contact is being added (Add another person pressed, or any of name/email/telephone supplied on a save) but Name is blank. Name is the ONLY mandatory part of a contact.
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test — the wording is DERIVED, not seen rendered. Admissible as `inferred` per the confidence taxonomy (legacy source + file:line). ALL CITATIONS BELOW RE-READ FIRST-HAND THIS PASS. /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/nominated_contact.js:28-32 — Joi.string().empty('').max(32) with STRING_EMPTY/ANY_REQUIRED -> requiredMessage(labels.NAME, labels.A); the conditional-required gate is at :45-58 (`.when(...)` then `Joi.object({ name: Joi.required() })`). Resolved through /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/messages/en.js:31-33 (`Enter ${article} ${label.toLowerCase()}`) with labels/en.js:2 (A: 'a') and labels/en.js:67 (NAME: 'Name') => "Enter a name".

validationMessages.message: Enter an email address or mobile number
validationMessages.trigger: A contact is being added with a Name but neither an Email address nor a Mobile number — at least one contact method is mandatory once a contact exists.
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test. Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:54-56 — `Joi.object({ name: Joi.required() }).or(EMAIL, TELEPHONE).messages({ [OBJECT_MISSING]: EMAIL_OR_TELEPHONE })`. LITERAL string (not derived) at validation/messages/en.js:73 — EMAIL_OR_TELEPHONE: 'Enter an email address or mobile number'. This is a fieldset-level (.or) error, so it has no single field to attach to — relevant to the rebuild given the page has no labels to hang inline errors from.

validationMessages.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com
validationMessages.trigger: Email address fails Joi email validation. Note ignoreLength:true is set, so length is policed only by the separate max(255) rule.
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test. Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:33-36 (`Joi.string().empty('').max(255).email({ ignoreLength: true })`, STRING_EMAIL -> EMAIL_CORRECT_FORMAT); LITERAL string at validation/messages/en.js:72. Matches the standard GOV.UK error-message pattern verbatim.

validationMessages.message: Enter a mobile number in the correct format, like 07700 900 982 or +44 7700 900 982
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test. Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:37-40 (`.pattern(TELEPHONE_REGEX)`, STRING_PATTERN -> TELEPHONE_NUMBER_CORRECT_FORMAT, imported from ../../../regexes at :4); LITERAL string at validation/messages/en.js:110. The regex body itself was NOT read — the exact accepted format (brackets? hyphens?) remains open. Confirmed separately from the trace: the input is type=tel with NO pattern attribute, so nothing constrains format client-side (action 50 attribute enumeration).

validationMessages.message: Name must be 32 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Name exceeds 32 characters. Server-side only — the input carries no maxlength attribute.
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test; DERIVED string. Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:28-29 — `.max(32)` with STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.NAME, 32); template at validation/messages/en.js:3-5 (`${label} must be ${maxLength} characters or fewer`) + labels/en.js:67 (NAME: 'Name') => "Name must be 32 characters or fewer". The ABSENCE of a client-side maxlength attribute is CONFIRMED (trace 219b882ee3b4fbe040b824d146d38991d6b70149 action 50, attribute enumeration re-run this pass) — so an over-length name is only caught on submit.

validationMessages.message: Email address must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Email address exceeds 255 characters. Server-side only — no maxlength attribute on the input.
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test; DERIVED string. Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:33-34 — `.max(255)` with STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.EMAIL_ADDRESS, 255); resolved via validation/messages/en.js:3-5 + labels/en.js:46 (EMAIL_ADDRESS: 'Email address'). Absence of maxlength on the input is confirmed from the trace.

validationMessages.message: Telephone number must be 30 characters or fewer
validationMessages.trigger: Mobile number exceeds 30 characters. Server-side only — no maxlength attribute on the input.
validationMessages.evidence: NOT observed in any trace and NOT asserted by any test; DERIVED string. Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:37-38 — `.max(30)` with STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.TELEPHONE_NUMBER, 30); resolved via validation/messages/en.js:3-5 + labels/en.js:98 (TELEPHONE_NUMBER: 'Telephone number') => "Telephone number must be 30 characters or fewer". THIS CONFIRMS THE COPY CONFLICT: the resolved message says 'Telephone number' while the rendered column header says 'Mobile number' (confirmed, action 50) and the fieldset-level error says 'mobile number' (messages/en.js:73). Three terms, one field.

validationMessages.message: "remove-nominated-contact" must be greater than or equal to 0 / "remove-nominated-contact" must be less than or equal to 4
validationMessages.trigger: A tampered/out-of-range Remove index is posted. Not user-facing copy — raw Joi default messages leaking the field name, reachable only by manipulating the form.
validationMessages.evidence: Re-read first-hand this pass: nominated_contact.js:41 — `Joi.number().min(0).max(4)` with no .messages() override, so Joi's defaults surface. Exact strings asserted in the legacy unit test /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/test/validation/routes/handlers/importer/nominated_contact_test.js:139 and :152 (both lines verified verbatim this pass). Included because the 0..4 bound is independent corroboration of the 5-contact cap (indices 0-4 = 5 contacts). The rebuild should NOT reproduce these raw Joi strings as user-facing copy.

openQuestions: *** CORRECTION MADE THIS PASS — THE EMPTY-CONTACT ANOMALY IS SOLVED, AND THE PREVIOUS DIAGNOSIS WAS WRONG. *** Earlier readings of this spec concluded that the blank contact row proved 'an empty contact object IS persisted on the draft — that much is now certain', called it a data-quality bug, and speculated it came from the draft-creation/seed path. That is FALSE and has been struck. The real cause is a one-line view-model default: transformers/notification_to_view/importer/nominated_contact.js:4 defaults nominatedContacts to `[{}]` (an array holding one empty object), while :5 defaults the SAME path to `[]` when computing showNominatedContactInput. So on a draft with NO contacts the template's {{#each}} still emits one blank removable row, and the entry row renders too — exactly the 2 tbody rows observed on both drafts. Nothing is persisted; the validator was never bypassed. This matters for the rebuild: the lesson is 'don't let the view model invent a record', not 'guard the datastore against empty contacts'. It also removes what looked like a contradiction with the validator (which does require name + one contact method).

CORROBORATION RESULT — SOURCE 2 ADDS NO NEW FIELDS, AND THAT IS ITSELF THE HEADLINE FINDING. The page object /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/notification/NominatedContactsPage.ts is 9 lines long and exposes exactly ONE locator: btnSaveAndContinue (lines 6-8). Re-read in full this pass — the claim is exact. There is no locator for name, email, telephone, Add another person or Remove. `grep -rni nominated` across the QA repo returns only: SEVEN workflow click-through call sites across THREE workflow files (ched-pp:448, ched-pp:871, ched-p:523, ched-p:795, ched-p:1001, ched-d:340, ched-d:660), three accessibility-spec click-throughs (ched-pp:124, ched-p:246, ched-d:167), the factory wiring (factory.ts:50 import, factory.ts:187 construction) and the page object itself. ChedPPNotificationConfig (ched-pp-workflows.ts:~41-129) has no nominated-contact field of any kind — notably it DOES carry billingEmail/billingTelephone (lines 127-128), so the omission is a deliberate scope choice, not an oversight of the config pattern. NET: the entire data-entry surface of this page is untested by QA automation. For this page the tests are a STRICTLY WEAKER source than the traces — they touch one button; the traces at least rendered the whole DOM.

NOTE ON SCOPE: ched-a-workflows.ts exists but contains NO reference to nominated contacts — so this page is reached by the CHED-PP, CHED-P and CHED-D journeys but apparently not by CHED-A (or CHED-A's coverage simply omits it). Only CHED-PP matters for the rebuild, but the three-way sharing is worth knowing: any change to this page's contract touches three journeys, not one.

SCOPE CONFLICT ON THE VALIDATION MESSAGES — RULED AND RE-TAGGED THIS PASS, no longer open. Successive passes flip-flopped on whether reading the IPAFFS application source is admissible, tagging the 8 validation messages `gap`, then `inferred`, then flagging the contradiction for a human. AUTHORISED-LEGACY-SOURCE PASS: the confidence taxonomy for THIS exercise defines a distinct `legacy` tier — "read from the authoritative IPAFFS source (validation message, model mandatoriness); cite file_path:line" — which sits ABOVE `inferred` in precedence (confirmed > legacy > inferred). Reading a message string out of nominated_contact.js + messages/en.js is squarely `legacy`, so all 8 messages have been re-tagged `inferred` -> `legacy` (an upgrade, not a downgrade). This pass opened nominated_contact.js, messages/en.js, labels/en.js, the transformer, nominatedContact.html and nominated_contact_test.js first-hand and verified EVERY cited line number and string: the four literals verbatim (messages/en.js:72 EMAIL_CORRECT_FORMAT, :73 EMAIL_OR_TELEPHONE, :110 TELEPHONE_NUMBER_CORRECT_FORMAT; the two raw Joi 0..4 defaults asserted in nominated_contact_test.js:139/:152), the four derived ones correctly composed from their templates (messages/en.js:3-5 maxLengthMessage, :31-33 requiredMessage) and labels (labels/en.js:2 A, :46 EMAIL_ADDRESS, :67 NAME, :98 TELEPHONE_NUMBER). The residual risk is unchanged and correctly stated: derived strings are what the code SHOULD produce, not what was SEEN rendered — a targeted error-state trace run would upgrade the 7 user-facing ones to `confirmed`.

MODEL-LEVEL MANDATORINESS — VERIFIED FROM THE JAVA SCHEMA THIS PASS (`legacy`), and it CONFIRMS the page is optional for CHED-PP at the model boundary. /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notificationschema/representation/PartOne.java:641-644 — `private List<NominatedContact> nominatedContacts` carries EXACTLY ONE constraint: `@NotEmpty(groups = NotificationTransporterContactDetailsEuFieldValidation.class)`. There is NO `@NotNull`/`@NotEmpty` for any CHED-PP validation group (contrast the immediately-preceding `contactDetails` field, which DOES carry `@NotNull(groups = NotificationChedppFieldValidation.class)` at PartOne.java:~633-639). So for CHED-PP the whole nominatedContacts list is optional server-side at the model layer — matching the H1 '(optional)' and the all-blank-submit-passes behaviour. The `@NotEmpty` that DOES exist is the transporter/high-risk-EU-CVEDA variant only, i.e. the same isHighRiskEuCveda branch the template swaps copy for — corroborating that 'you must add at least one transporter contact' is a DIFFERENT journey's rule, not CHED-PP's. NominatedContact.java:15-21 declares three plain `String` fields (name, telephone, email) with ZERO validation annotations — so name-required + one-contact-method is NOT a model constraint either; it lives ONLY in the frontend Joi conditional-add gate (nominated_contact.js:45-58). NET for the rebuild: at the model level a CHED-PP notification may legitimately have zero nominated contacts and, if the Java layer alone were trusted, a contact with no name; the 'name + one method when adding' rule is a frontend-only policy the rebuild must consciously re-implement (or move server-side), not inherit for free.

RECOMMENDED ACTION (unchanged, still worth doing): a targeted trace run submitting a partial contact (name only; email only; bad email; bad phone; over-length name) would upgrade all 7 user-facing messages from `inferred` to `confirmed` and capture the error-summary heading + inline error placement, which source alone cannot give. The page object needs three fill locators added. The rebuild is NOT blocked on it — the rules themselves are documented with verified citations.

VALIDATION-DESIGN FLAG FOR THE REBUILD: the phone field is validated with `.pattern(TELEPHONE_REGEX)` and capped at 30 chars, i.e. it accepts any telephone-shaped value — it does NOT enforce a mobile, despite the column header saying 'Mobile number' and the stated purpose being to notify contacts if the consignment is picked for inspection. If notification is by SMS, a landline entered here silently fails to reach anyone. Worth a product decision in the new app: either enforce mobile, or stop promising notification, or accept and say so.

The page is OPTIONAL and this is doubly confirmed: the H1 says "(optional)", and the CHED-PP workflows (ched-pp:448, :871) plus the CHED-P (:523/795/1001), CHED-D (:340/660) and all three accessibility specs click Save and continue with every field blank and the journey proceeds. Independently confirmed from the traces themselves: in BOTH observed traces every input has value="" at click time, and the action-50 log records the click navigating on to /notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1525990/documents/page-1. An all-blank submit is definitively a valid, passing state.

AXE-CLEAN BUT NOT DS-COMPLIANT — a genuinely useful negative result. ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:123 runs a full axe WCAG 2.1 A/AA scan on this page, and accessibility-utils.ts:21-22 sets maxViolations: 0 / failOnViolations: true. Since the corpus is overwhelmingly passing, this page has ZERO automated a11y violations — despite having no <label> on any input (confirmed: 0 label elements in main, in both traces), a table used as form layout, and a hint double-referenced by both the fieldset and the table. axe accepts aria-labelledby pointing at a <th> as a valid accessible name. Do NOT let 'the legacy page passes axe' be used as an argument to replicate the pattern: the DS non-compliance and the mobile-reflow fragility are real and simply invisible to axe.

THE 5-CONTACT CAP — MECHANISM RESOLVED FROM SOURCE (`inferred`), citations verified first-hand this pass. It IS enforced by silently withholding the controls: transformers/notification_to_view/importer/nominated_contact.js:5 sets `showNominatedContactInput: _.get(notification, 'partOne.nominatedContacts', []).length < 5`, and the template gates BOTH the entry row (nominatedContact.html:90) and the 'Add another person' button (nominatedContact.html:131) on that one flag. Independently corroborated by the validator's `Joi.number().min(0).max(4)` on the Remove index (nominated_contact.js:41) — indices 0-4, i.e. 5 contacts. So at capacity the user simply finds the entry row and the Add button GONE, with no message explaining why: the only mention of the limit is the body copy 'Nominate up to 5 contacts'. Still unobserved (no trace ever saved a real contact). OPEN FOR THE REBUILD as a design question: silent disappearance is poor UX and GOV.UK would normally state the limit and/or explain the removal.

COPY CONFLICT — CONFIRMED TO EXIST IN SOURCE, re-verified this pass. ONE field carries THREE different names: the rendered column header says 'Mobile number' (confirmed, trace 219b882e... action 50, th enumeration); the HTML input name/id is `telephone` (confirmed, same action); and the max-length error resolves to 'Telephone number must be 30 characters or fewer' via labels/en.js:98 (TELEPHONE_NUMBER: 'Telephone number'), while the fieldset-level error says 'Enter an email address or mobile number' (messages/en.js:73). So the SAME validator module refers to the same field as both 'Telephone number' and 'mobile number' depending on which rule fires. The user-visible drift is inferred rather than seen rendered, but it is not speculative — it is legible in the source. The rebuild should pick one term; 'Mobile number' fits the stated purpose — but see the validation-design flag above, since the validation does not actually enforce a mobile.

Add and Remove are both full server round-trips (submit buttons posting the form), not client-side row manipulation — confirmed from markup, never exercised. The rebuild should decide deliberately whether to keep the no-JS round-trip model (progressive-enhancement friendly, matches the DS 'Add another' pattern) or move to client-side rows.

SHARED TEMPLATE — CONFIRMED FROM SOURCE THIS PASS (`inferred`); the previous pass withdrew this to UNVERIFIED, and that withdrawal is now itself reversed. The template read was repeated first-hand and the variant IS real: views/importer/nominatedContact.html:23-35 branches on `{{#if isHighRiskEuCveda}}` to swap the page title to primaryTitle="Transporter Contact Details" / secondaryTitle="Transport" (else primaryTitle="Nominated contacts (optional)" / secondaryTitle="Contacts"), and :39-43 injects an extra paragraph "You must add at least one transporter's contact details." — flipping the section from optional to mandatory. NOTE the 'Nominate up to 5 contacts...' hint sits OUTSIDE that branch (:44-46), so the transporter variant shows BOTH paragraphs. Everything below the legend (table, three inputs, Add/Remove, save buttons) is identical across both variants. For CHED-PP the branch is always false, which is why both traces render 'Nominated contacts (optional)'. SEPARATELY — the previous pass was RIGHT to withdraw the claim that the QA repo's TransportContactDetailsPage.ts is a page object over this same template: that file exposes `radioNotifyContactsNo` = getByRole('radio', { name: 'No' }), and this page has ZERO radios (trace 219b882e... action 50 enumerated every input/select/textarea/button: three text inputs, two hidden inputs, four buttons, no radios — re-verified this pass). It is driven only by ched-a-workflows.ts:441-443 and :1264-1265 and is a different, CHED-A-only page. Both things are true: the isHighRiskEuCveda variant of THIS template is real, and TransportContactDetailsPage is not it. IMPLICATION FOR THE REBUILD: two journeys with different optionality and different copy are entangled in one template, and the mandatory variant is enforced by a paragraph of prose rather than by validation — nominated_contact.js has no isHighRiskEuCveda concept at all, so 'you must add at least one' appears to be UNENFORCED server-side. Do not inherit the coupling.

SCOPE NOTE ON THE VALIDATOR (verified first-hand this pass): nominated_contact.js validates ONE contact at a time (flat name/email/telephone keys), not an array — consistent with the observed single entry row and the server round-trip add model. The saved list lives at partOne.nominatedContacts and is only ever appended to via a POST. So there is no cross-contact validation (e.g. duplicate email detection) anywhere in this handler — worth deciding deliberately in the rebuild whether duplicates should be caught.

Both evidence pointers verified correct in this adversarial pass: action 50 in trace 219b882e... (ched-pp-article72.spec.ts:15, 0 errors) and action 58 in trace e10b7cd5... (ched-pp-hmi-checks.spec.ts:15, 0 errors) are both the 'Save and continue' click on this page, and their snapshots capture the page pre-navigation. Both render H1 'Nominated contacts (optional)' and title 'Nominated Contacts - Import and export applications - GOV.UK' — right page, no doubt. Minor CLI note: `trace snapshot <id> --name before` errors with 'No snapshot found' even though `trace action <id>` lists before/input/after as available; the bare `trace snapshot <id>` form works and returns the input-phase snapshot. Also note `--filename=` output is JSON-escaped (attributes appear as class=\"x\"), so grepping it for raw `attr="value"` gives FALSE NEGATIVES — use `-- eval` with attribute enumeration instead.
